summary: Six suspects split three ways: k8k and lio are genuinely cross-repo (fix/AC lives in Sinnix and Sinex respectively) and must not be closed from inside Polylogue; 6mv/fs1/7aw are correctly Polylogue-owned but carry an explicit downstream seam whose companion work lives in the sibling repos and is not cross-referenced; lnd is clean (assertions-vs-beads is a purely Polylogue-internal doctrine). Sinex's own tracker already holds the companion beads (sinex-4j2 family, sinex-hlv, sinex-zi6, sinex-266), but the Polylogue beads do not point at them, so the seams are real yet invisible from Polylogue's side. Sinnix has no issues.jsonl tracker surface, so k8k's remediation half is untracked anywhere.

inconsistencies.evidence: Bug is typed/filed in Polylogue but the remediation lives in Sinnix: description says 'The wrapper is generated by /realm/project/sinnix/scripts/sinnix-direnvrc and routes polylogue through sinnix-scope background' (file confirmed present, 7532 bytes). Acceptance demands 'adjust Sinnix/devenv scoping so lightweight Polylogue CLI reads avoid the extra latency' — that edit is a Sinnix change, not a Polylogue one. No companion bead exists in Sinnix (its .beads/ has no issues.jsonl; grep for polylogue/scope/direnvrc/latency found none).
inconsistencies.fix: Split the bead: keep a Polylogue 'measure/classify latency' half (before/after timing is doable here), and open a Sinnix bead owning the sinnix-direnvrc scoping change. Cross-reference. Acceptance clause 'adjust Sinnix/devenv scoping' cannot be met without a Sinnix commit and should be moved out of the Polylogue acceptance surface.

inconsistencies.evidence: Title and body are self-declaring cross-repo: 'Align cross-repo devloop contract on beads (Sinex parity)' and 'Update the shared contract in BOTH repos ... Sinex still uses devloop-checkpoint --queue as its directive channel.' The devloop-checkpoint tooling lives in Sinex; extending its contract-drift check to beads presence is a Sinex code change. Sinex already tracks the receiving half as sinex-hlv ('Ingest beads and devloop as Sinex sources'), but lio references no Sinex bead.
inconsistencies.fix: This bead's acceptance cannot be satisfied from the Polylogue checkout alone. Add a cross-reference to the Sinex-side bead (sinex-hlv, or a dedicated Sinex 'devloop-checkpoint beads parity' bead if hlv is scoped to source-ingestion only) and narrow lio's own acceptance to the Polylogue-side contract text, delegating the Sinex conductor change to the companion.

inconsistencies.evidence: Decision correctly owned by Polylogue (defines what Polylogue emits), but body states 'the Sinex-side emitter is future work gated on ... Sinex production restore' with no pointer. Sinex already tracks the consuming/emitting half: sinex-4j2 'Polylogue on Sinex: evidential substrate', sinex-4j2.3 'Phase B: Sinex-primary evidence', and sinex-zi6 'Producer protocol as a public contract' (which notes 'polylogue already publishes EventIntent to NATS ... crate/sinexd/src/sources/source_contracts/polylogue.rs'). The seam is clear but uncited.
inconsistencies.fix: Add a neutral cross-reference from 6mv to sinex-4j2 / sinex-zi6 so the doctrine bead names its downstream implementation home. No re-parenting; 6mv stays a Polylogue decision.

inconsistencies.evidence: 'Upstream native integration: lifecycle hooks / polylogue-hook support in the open-source Hermes agent' — acceptance ('contribute generic lifecycle-hook support ... upstream') can only be met by a PR to the external Hermes repository, which is not a sibling tracker (Sinex/Lynchpin/Sinnix) and not the Polylogue tree. The rest of the fs1 epic (state.db intake fs1.1, ATOF/ATIF spans fs1.2) is genuine Polylogue source-family work through the normal acquire->detect->parse->store pipeline and is correctly scoped.
inconsistencies.fix: Label fs1.7 as external-upstream-dependency and gate it explicitly (it can neither be verified nor landed inside any local repo). Do not treat fs1 as blocked on it. fs1's Polylogue<->Sinex seam is otherwise clean because it defers to 6mv.

inconsistencies.evidence: Bulk is legitimate Polylogue work (config-artifact source family, content-hash versioning, session_agent_policies join — all Polylogue substrate). But one acceptance clause — 'one state-like global-CLAUDE.md section migrated to injected-equivalent ... and the static text retired' — requires editing Sinnix-owned files. Design acknowledges the seam: 'edit affordances route to the owning files (sinnix dots) — no shadow editing.' The retirement commit is a Sinnix change with no companion bead.
inconsistencies.fix: Keep 7aw in Polylogue; the capture/versioning/classification acceptance is Polylogue-native. Carve the 'retire a static CLAUDE.md section in sinnix dots' step into a Sinnix companion bead (or explicit operator step) so the Polylogue AC does not silently require a Sinnix commit.

inconsistencies.evidence: 'SessionStart preamble opt-in rollout (polylogue + sinnix repos)' — acceptance wires compose_context_preamble into SessionStart hooks 'per-project via .claude/settings.json, polylogue + sinnix first'. The sinnix half edits the Sinnix repo. Sinex tracks adjacent ambient-injection work (sinex-cfr, sinex-7z0.3) but the Sinnix-repo settings wiring is untracked.
inconsistencies.fix: Polylogue-repo wiring stays here; note that the sinnix-repo .claude/settings.json edit is a separate deployment/config change owned by Sinnix, and either add a Sinnix companion bead or record it as an explicit operator rollout step so the AC is not half-external.

reorg.rationale: Root cause and remediation are in /realm/project/sinnix/scripts/sinnix-direnvrc (sinnix-scope wrapper). Only the before/after timing/classification is Polylogue-local. No Sinnix companion bead exists (Sinnix .beads has no issues.jsonl surface), so the remediation is currently untracked anywhere.

Self-declared BOTH-repos change; the Sinex conductor (devloop-checkpoint --queue) is Sinex-owned. Sinex already has sinex-hlv as the receiving-side bead; add the cross-reference so the twins do not drift silently again (which is the bead's own stated fear).

Decision stays in Polylogue; annotate that the Sinex-side emitter/consumer is already tracked in the sinex-4j2 family and sinex-zi6, so the 'future work' is not actually untracked, only uncited. sinex-266 explicitly self-labels as 'Sinex-side half of a cross-repo pair' — same model 6mv should adopt.

Acceptance requires an upstream contribution to a third-party repository with no local tracker; mark as external-dependency so it cannot silently block the otherwise-Polylogue-native fs1 importer children.

Source-family ingestion is Polylogue; the 'retire a static global-CLAUDE.md section' acceptance clause edits Sinnix dots. Split that clause out to a Sinnix step so the Polylogue AC is self-satisfiable.
